content.cn_text: 在本节中，你将听到两位学生讨论他们将要参加的幼儿辅导课程
content.en_text: In this section you will hear two students discussing the Early Childhood Tutorial they are going to

content.cn_text: 礼物。
content.en_text: present.

content.cn_text: 首先，看看问题21至问题26。
content.en_text: First, look at Questions 21 to 26.

content.cn_text: (短暂的停顿)
content.en_text: [SHORT PAUSE]

content.cn_text: 现在听讨论的第一部分，回答问题21至问题26。
content.en_text: Now listen to the first part of the discussion and answer Questions 21 to 26.

content.cn_text: 玛丽:我想你还没有想到我们的教程演示的点子，是吗?
content.en_text: Marie: I don't suppose you've come up with an idea for our tutorial presentation, have you?

content.cn_text: 柔丝:嗯，事实上，我想我们可以谈谈在孩子成长过程中我们看到的两性之间的明显差异。
content.en_text: Rose: Well, as a matter of fact, I have - I thought we could talk about the obvious differences we see between the sexes as children grow up.

content.cn_text: 玛丽:你是说我们看到的男性和女性之间的差异是由他们的成长方式造成的吗?
content.en_text: Marie: Do you mean the differences we see between males and females as a result of the way they are brought up?

content.cn_text: 柔丝:不，我是说从出生起就存在的差异。
content.en_text: Rose: No, I mean the differences that exist from birth.

content.cn_text: 玛丽:听起来工作量很大，罗斯……
content.en_text: Marie: That sounds like a lot of work, Rose ...

content.cn_text: 罗斯:不完全是，你还记得在我们的第一次早期儿童讲座中，我们得到了一张在英国观察到的男性和女性婴儿和幼儿的差异清单吗?
content.en_text: Rose: Not really - do you remember in our first Early Childhood lecture, we were:given a list of differences which were observed in male and female babies and toddlers in the UK?

content.cn_text: 玛丽:这学期的前两周我都没来，记得吗?我遇到了问题
content.en_text: Marie: I wasn't here for the first two weeks of the semester remember? I had problems

content.cn_text: 拿我的护照。
content.en_text: getting my passport.

content.cn_text: 柔丝:哦，没错。嗯，真的很吸引人。英国的一组行为科学家挑选了100个孩子，对他们进行了20到25年的长期观察。他们成长的家庭对男孩和女孩一视同仁——没有对任何一个性别的特殊对待。他们观察他们的比赛和他们对各种情况的反应——我想是设置了一些小测试。
content.en_text: Rose: Oh, that's right. Well, it was really fascinating. A group of behavioural scientists in England selected 100 children to observe over a very long period - 20 or 25 years.They were brought up in families who treated girls and boys in the same way - no special treatment for either of the sexes. They observed their play and their reactions to various situations - set up little tests I suppose.

content.cn_text: 玛丽:孩子们多大了?
content.en_text: Marie: How old were the children?

content.cn_text: 柔丝:第一个;观察是在婴儿只有几个小时大的时候进行的，他们得出的结论是，在那个年龄，女孩比男孩对触摸更敏感!
content.en_text: Rose: The first; observations were carried out when the babies were only a few hours old - they concluded that girls were more sensitive to touch than boys at that early age!

content.cn_text: 玛丽:他们是怎么得出这个结论的?
content.en_text: Marie: How did they end up with that conclusion?

content.cn_text: 柔丝:嗯，讲师没有详细讲，我想他只是想引起我们的兴趣，你知道，勾起我们的胃口。从出生后不久，一直到他们二十出头，都进行了大量的测试和观察。我想我们可以调查一些案例研究，然后在教程中展示结果。
content.en_text: Rose: Well, the lecturer didn't go into detail -I think he just wanted to get our interest,you know, whet our appetite. There were lots of tests and observations done from soon after birth, right through to their early twenties. I thought we could investigate some of the case studies and then present the results in the tutorial.

content.cn_text: 玛丽:好主意，罗斯。这会很有趣，但它也会给我们机会为期末作业收集信息。
content.en_text: Marie: That's a good idea Rose. It'll be interesting but it will also give us the chance to collect information for our end-of-term assignment as well.

content.cn_text: 柔丝:这也是一个很好的机会去查阅图书馆的可用资源。我还没有机会在那里待很久，你呢?
content.en_text: Rose: It'll also be a good opportunity to check out the resources available in the library. I haven't had the chance to spend much time there yet, have you?

content.cn_text: 玛丽:过去的四个星期太忙了，当然，我必须补上我错过的两个星期，我也没有机会。我听说图书馆的研究人员很愿意帮忙。
content.en_text: Marie: The last four weeks have just been so busy - and of course, I had to catch up on the two weeks that I missed -I haven't had the chance either. I've heard that the library research staff are really willing to help out.

content.cn_text: 柔丝:嗯，我们可以看看这是不是真的。我们需要预约才能见他们。显然，他们的需求量很大。
content.en_text: Rose: Well, we can find out if that's true or not. We'll need to make an appointment to see them. Apparently they're in high demand.

content.cn_text: 玛丽:我们只有两周的时间来准备这个教程，所以我想我们应该尽快开始。今天下午我们去见导师，把我们的计划告诉他。如果他同意，我们就可以开始研究了。
content.en_text: Marie: We only have two weeks to prepare for this tutorial, so I think we should definitely start as soon as we can. Let's see the tutor this afternoon and tell him about our plan. If he agrees, we can get started on our research.

content.cn_text: 柔丝:好的，我去找家教。你可以在图书馆预约。
content.en_text: Rose: OK - I'll go and see the tutor. You can make a booking at the library.

content.cn_text: 罗丝去导师办公室讨论辅导课的题目。
content.en_text: Rose goes to the tutor's office to discuss the topic for their tutorial.

content.cn_text: 在听剩下的对话内容之前，请看问题27至问题30。
content.en_text: Before listening to the rest of the conversation, look at Questions 27 to 30.

content.cn_text: (SHORTPAUSE)
content.en_text: [SHORTPAUSE]

content.cn_text: 柔丝:可以见一下吉姆·克拉克吗?他是幼儿辅导老师之一。
content.en_text: Rose: Would it be possible to see Jim Clark - one of the Early Childhood tutors?

content.cn_text: AA:我可以问一下是关于什么的吗?
content.en_text: AA: May I ask what it's about?

content.cn_text: (行政助理)
content.en_text: (Admin Assistant)

content.cn_text: 柔丝:我们的EC 101辅导课题目必须得到批准。
content.en_text: Rose: We have to get approval for our tutorial topics in EC 101.

content.cn_text: AA:是的，我想可能是关于这个的。不幸的是，吉姆这周要去悉尼，但他给了我一些关于教程的具体问题。
content.en_text: AA: Yes, I thought it might be about that. Unfortunately, Jim had to go to Sydney this week but he has given me some specific questions to ask about the tutorials.

content.cn_text: 柔丝:哦，我们希望能开始我们的研究，我们只有两个星期的时间。
content.en_text: Rose: Oh, we were hoping to get started on our research - we've only got two weeks.

content.cn_text: 别担心，吉姆一天打两次电话。如果你告诉我细节，我可以在明天早上之前给你答复。
content.en_text: AA: Don't worry - Jim's phoning in twice a day. If you give me the details, I can give you an answer by tomorrow morning.

content.cn_text: 柔丝:太好了。我们计划介绍一些案例研究，这些研究是由一组……
content.en_text: Rose: That's great. We are planning to present some case studies that were undertaken by a group of...

content.cn_text: AA:等等。我只需要一些简短的细节——让我看看，我必须写下教程的主题是什么。
content.en_text: AA: Hang on. I just need a few short details - let me see, I have to write down what the subject of the tutorial is.

content.cn_text: 柔丝:好，我想话题是性别，以及两性什么时候开始表现不同。
content.en_text: Rose: OK - I guess the topic is gender and when the sexes start to act differently.

content.cn_text: AA:所以，这是关于男孩和女孩的不同吗?我可以在“主题”旁边写些什么?
content.en_text: AA: So, is it about how male and female children are different? What can I write here,next to TOPIC?

content.cn_text: 柔丝:嗯，那“两性如何不同”呢?
content.en_text: Rose: Well, what about “How the sexes differ"?

content.cn_text: AA:好的，我把它作为你的主题。吉姆也想知道你的教程的目的。
content.en_text: AA: OK - I'll put that down as your topic. Jim also wants to know the aim of your tutorial.

content.cn_text: 柔丝:嗯，我想有两个目的。首先是展示它们的不同之处。但我们想说的另一点是，这些差异是天生的，而不是后天习得的。
content.en_text: Rose: Well, there are two aims I suppose. The first is to show how they differ. But the other point we want to make is that the differences are innate - not learned.

content.cn_text: 为了表明两性之间的差异是天生的，而不是后天习得的。对，这是最难的部分。现在我需要知道你辅导课的日期，时间和房间。
content.en_text: AA: To - show - that - differences - between - the - sexes - are -- innate - not learned. Right - that's the hard part. Now I need to know the date, time and room of your tutorial.

content.cn_text: 柔丝:两周后，让我想想，是26号星期二上午11点。我们在B1203房间。
content.en_text: Rose: It's in two weeks - let's see, that'll be Tuesday 26th at 11 a.m. We are in Room B1203,

content.cn_text: AA:您需要影音材料吗?
content.en_text: AA: And do you need any A/V material?

content.cn_text: 柔丝:AV是什么意思?
content.en_text: Rose: What does AV mean?

content.cn_text: AA:视听设备——你知道，电视、录像机、录音机、投影仪之类的东西。
content.en_text: AA: Audio-visual - you know, TV, video, tape-recorder, overhead projector - that kind of thing.

content.cn_text: 柔丝:我还没想过这个，我想我们需要一个投影仪。我们还没有真正开始计划我们的教程-我们只是想得到吉姆的初步批准。
content.en_text: Rose: I hadn't thought of that - guess we'll need an overhead, projector. We haven't really started planning our tutorial yet - we just wanted to get initial approval from Jim.

content.cn_text: AA:没关系，如果你不需要投影仪，你可以取消它。吉姆早上会打电话来——你想那时来看我吗?或者如果你愿意，我可以打电话给你。
content.en_text: AA: Never mind - you can always cancel the projector if you don't need it. Jim will phone in the morning - do you want to come and see me then or I can phone you if you like.

content.cn_text: 柔丝:我明天早上8点到10点有课，所以下课后我会过来。
content.en_text: Rose: I have a lecture from 8 to 10 tomorrow morning - so I'll drop by after it finishes.

content.cn_text: AA:好的，到时候见。
content.en_text: AA: Right - I'll see you then.

content.cn_text: 以上是第三部分的全部内容，现在你有半分钟的时间检查答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of Section 3, You now have 1/2, minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: 现在翻到听力问题手册的第四部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to Section 4 of your Listening Question Booklet.

groups.desc:
Questions 21-22
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: Rose's plan for the tutorial is to research and present

options.title: A
options.content: the differences between mule and female children.

options.title: B
options.content: the different ways that male and female children are raised

options.title: C
options.content: the differences between mole and female paientiug.

items.analysis: Rose明确表示她想讨论"随着孩子们长大，我们观察到的男女之间明显的差异"。这直接对应了选项A中提到的"男性和女性儿童之间的差异"。此外，Rose后续强调这些差异是"从出生就存在的"，而不是后天培养的结果，进一步确认了她的计划是研究和展示男女儿童之间的固有差异。

items.title: The British experiments that Rose refers to in the conversation

options.title: B
options.content: observed girls and boys who worn raised identically.

options.title: C
options.content: noted the different treatments males and females received.

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B，因为原文明确指出实验中的男孩和女孩被以相同的方式对待，没有特殊待遇。这正是"raised identically"（以相同方式抚养）的含义。实验的目的是观察在相同条件下成长的男孩和女孩，而不是记录他们受到的不同对待或仅仅在100个家庭中进行观察。

groups.desc:
Questions 23-24
Choose TWO letters from A-E for each answer.
groups.title: This tutorial will

list.title: A
list.content: require Mario and Rose to carry out research

list.title: B
list.content: interest the tutor and their classmates.

list.title: D
list.content: help Marie to catch up mi her research work.

list.title: E
list.content: benefit other assessment that Rose and Marie have to do.

items.analysis: 正确答案是A和E，因为对话中明确提到了他们需要进行研究（"investigate some of the case studies"），这对应了选项A。同时，Marie提到这个教程也会帮助他们收集期末作业的信息（"give us the chance to collect information for our end-of-term assignment"），这直接对应了选项E。这两个选项最准确地反映了对话中提到的教程的目的和好处。

正确答案是A和E，因为对话中明确提到了他们需要进行研究（"investigate some of the case studies"），这对应了选项A。同时，Marie提到这个教程也会帮助他们收集期末作业的信息（"give us the chance to collect information for our end-of-term assignment"），这直接对应了选项E。这两个选项最准确地反映了对话中提到的教程的目的和好处。

groups.desc:
Questions 25-26
Choose TWO letters from A-E for each answer.
groups.title: Before starting their preparation, Marie and Rose must

collect_options.title: Before starting their preparation, Marie and Rose must

list.title: D
list.content: make an appointment to see a librarian.

list.title: E
list.content: plan carefully for the next two weeks.

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C和D，因为在开始准备他们的演讲之前，Marie和Rose必须先获得导师的批准（C）并预约图书馆员（D）。对话中明确提到他们需要见导师获得批准，以及需要预约图书馆员的帮助。这两个步骤都是在他们开始实际准备工作之前必须完成的。
这道题的正确答案是C和D，因为在开始准备他们的演讲之前，Marie和Rose必须先获得导师的批准（C）并预约图书馆员（D）。对话中明确提到他们需要见导师获得批准，以及需要预约图书馆员的帮助。这两个步骤都是在他们开始实际准备工作之前必须完成的。

groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
Complete the form below.
Use NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.

content:
Topic: 'How The Sexes Differ'
Aims of tutorial: 
1. To show$17552$
2. To show the differences are$17553$Day & Date of tutorial: Tuesday 26th

Choose the correct letter from A-D.
How do you want to be notified of lecturer's approval?$17554$
A PHONE   
B LETTER   
C FAX   
D IN PERSON

items.analysis: 这道题的关键在于理解"EC 101"中的"EC"是什么的缩写。在早期儿童教育的相关课程中，EC通常代表"Early Childhood"（早期儿童）。题目要求填写的是部门名称，而不是具体的讨论主题。虽然对话中讨论了性别差异这个具体主题，但这不是问题所询问的。正确答案"Early Childhood"是基于对EC 101这个课程代码的理解。记住，在学术环境中，课程代码通常包含学科的缩写。所以，即使你没有直接听到"Early Childhood"这个词，也可以通过EC这个缩写推断出正确答案。这个题目考察了你对学术环境中常见缩写的理解能力，以及从上下文中提取关键信息的能力。

这道题的关键在于准确捕捉对话中最终确定的主题。虽然Rose最初提到了一个较长的描述（"gender and when the sexes start to act differently"），但管理助理要求一个更简洁的表述。当你听到"What can I write here, next to TOPIC?"这个问题时，这是一个明确的信号，表明接下来的内容就是我们需要填写的答案。Rose回答"Well, what about 'How the sexes differ'?"，这就是最终确定的主题。所以正确答案是"the differences"或"how they differ"。记住，在填空题中，我们通常需要使用原文中的词语，而不是自己的改写。因此，即使你理解了主题是关于性别差异，也要使用原文中的表达方式来回答。

这道题的关键在于理解和识别讲话者提到的第二个目的。文中明确说明了两个目的，第一个是展示性别差异，而第二个才是我们需要填写的答案。讲话者使用"the other point"这个短语来引出第二个目的，这是一个重要的信号词。接着，她明确表示这些差异是"innate - not learned"，即先天的，而非后天学习的。这正是我们需要填入的答案。记住，在听力考试中，当听到类似"另一点"、"第二个原因"等表达时，往往意味着重要信息即将出现。此外，"innate"和"learned"形成了一个对比，这种对比结构通常包含关键信息。因此，准确捕捉这个对比是回答这个问题的关键。

这道题的正确答案是D（IN PERSON），即Rose将亲自来获知讲师的批准。之所以选择这个答案，是因为在对话的最后部分，管理助理说："Right - I'll see you then"，而Rose之前表示她会在第二天早上的讲座结束后过来。这明确表示Rose将亲自来办公室获知结果，而不是通过电话、信件或传真。

关于选项分析：
A（PHONE）：虽然管理助理提到可以给Rose打电话，但Rose选择了亲自来。
B（LETTER）和C（FAX）：对话中完全没有提到这两种通知方式。
D（IN PERSON）：正确答案，与对话内容相符。

这个问题考察了学生对对话细节的理解能力，特别是对于对话结尾部分的注意力。它强调了在听力考试中，有时关键信息可能出现在对话的最后，因此学生需要保持专注直到对话结束。
